title: Local Networking, Name Resolution, and Secure Remote Access · System Forensics
summary: Restore access to a host by testing local configuration, route, name resolution, listening sockets, and SSH trust in causal order. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.
Trace hosts files, resolver configuration, search domains, DNS queries, caches, and application lookup results.
Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Remote Copy Tunnel evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Remote Copy Tunnel assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Remote Copy Tunnel has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Remote Copy Tunnel precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: SSH port forwarding exposes only harmless local traffic by definition.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

Inspect the Interface Route Socket trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

content.text: For Interface Route Socket, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Interface Route Socket precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-remote-copy-tunnel-oh93fh" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Remote Copy Tunnel into observable performance evidence.

steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Remote Copy Tunnel: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.
